system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]

  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Hospital Billing / Invoice for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Telugu (code=te, script=Telugu). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Andhra Pradesh, district=East Godavari.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Telugu for Telugu.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 32
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Tobacco Curer
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Andhra Pradesh
- district: East Godavari
- first_language: Telugu
- clinical_persona_summary: Mr. Enkateshwarlu Atloori, a 32-year-old male tobacco curer, is married with a matriculation education and works in an urban setting in East Godavari, Andhra Pradesh. He speaks Telugu and has limited English proficiency, and his lifestyle includes occasional health concerns and a love for folk music. His family follows a traditional joint system and celebrates festivals like Ugadi and Sankranti.

Document format guide for Hospital Billing / Invoice (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Invoice — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | MRN [[MRN|...]]
Address district [[DISTRICT|...]] PIN [[PIN_CODE|110001]]
Ambulance / parking vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Line items with amounts (no entity tags on procedure names).

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PIN_CODE — ZIP / Postal Code (PIN Code) e.g. [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- TAX_ID — Tax Identification Number (TIN/GSTIN) e.g. [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
- TELEPHONE_LANDLINE — Telephone Number (Landline) e.g. [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Urdu (code=ur, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Odisha, district=Khordha.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Urdu.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 49
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Odisha
- district: Khordha
- first_language: Urdu
- clinical_persona_summary: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old female who is currently married and retired, residing in an urban area of Odisha. She is illiterate, speaks Urdu as her first language, and lives in Khordha district. Her lifestyle is characterized by a focus on household management and community involvement, with a noted love for traditional cooking and a structured daily routine.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ریفرل [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College & Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Subrata Patnaik]] سے
بابت: [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]], [[AGE|49]] / [[GENDER|Female]], ضلع [[DISTRICT|Khordha]]

وجہ: مریضہ 3 ماہ سے دائیں انگوئنل ہرنیا (inguinal hernia) کے درد اور ناقابلِ تخفیف (irreducible) ہونے کے ساتھ علاماتِ آنتوں کی رکاوٹ (bowel obstruction) کے ساتھ پیش ہو رہی ہیں۔

مریضہ 49 سالہ ناخواندہ گھریلو خاتون ہیں جو کھوردھا ضلع، اوڈیشہ سے ہیں۔ وہ گزشتہ تین ماہ سے دائیں ران کے حصے میں بتدریج بڑھتے ہوئے سوجن کی اطلاع دیتی ہیں، جو گزشتہ ایک ہفتے سے دردناک اور ناقابلِ تخفیف ہو گئی ہے۔ وہ متلی، قے اور قبض کی شکایت کرتی ہیں۔ معائنے پر، دائیں انگوئنل خطے میں ایک دردناک اور ناقابلِ تخفیف گانٹھ (mass) موجود ہے۔ مریضہ ہیمو ڈائنامک طور پر مستحکم (hemodynamically stable) ہے۔

طبی شبہ کی بنیاد پر کہ ہرنیا میں رکاوٹ ہے، جراحی مداخلت (surgical intervention) کی ضرورت ہے۔ مریضہ کو مزید تشخیص اور انتظام کے لیے ایس سی بی میڈیکل کالج اینڈ ہسپتال کے شعبہ جراحت (Department of Surgery) میں ریفر کیا جاتا ہے۔ ان کا [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] حوالہ کے لیے منسلک ہے۔

مزید معلومات کے لیے، براہ کرم ریفر کرنے والے معالج سے [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437012345]] پر رابطہ کریں۔ مریضہ کے شوہر، [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Tandi]]، ان کے ساتھ ہوں گے۔ ان کے [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P12345678]] اور [[DRIVING_LICENCE|OD14 20100012345]] شناختی مقاصد کے لیے دستیاب ہیں۔
generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College & Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Subrata Patnaik]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]], [[AGE|49]] / [[GENDER|Female]], District [[DISTRICT|Khordha]]
Reason: Patient presents with a painful, irreducible right inguinal hernia of 3 months duration, with symptoms of bowel obstruction.

Patient is a 49-year-old illiterate female homemaker from Khordha district, Odisha. She reports a history of a gradually enlarging swelling in the right groin region over the past three months, which has become painful and non-reducible over the last week. She complains of nausea, vomiting, and constipation. On examination, there is a tender, irreducible mass in the right inguinal region. The patient is hemodynamically stable.

Given the clinical suspicion of an obstructed hernia, surgical intervention is required. The patient is referred to the Department of Surgery at SCB Medical College & Hospital for further evaluation and management. Her [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] is attached for reference.

For any further information, please contact the referring physician at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437012345]]. The patient's husband, [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Tandi]], will accompany her. Her [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P12345678]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|OD14 20100012345]] are available for identification.
judge_reasoning: Urdu Arabic-script prose correct; all TYPEs in allow-list; female 49yo hernia case fits persona/domain; no script or entity issues.

professional_persona: Minati Tandi is a seasoned household manager and aspiring home‑catering entrepreneur who meticulously plans meals, budgets the family expenses, and coordinates community celebrations, all while her innate need for structure sometimes leads her to over‑prepare and stress over every detail.
linguistic_persona: Minati Tandi speaks fluent Urdu at home, comfortably converses in Hindi, and, being illiterate, relies on family members to handle any reading or writing in Odia, Urdu or Hindi, which she navigates with a sharp memory and a habit of double‑checking spoken instructions.
sports_persona: Minati Tandi enjoys watching cricket, especially rooting for the Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, joins the local women’s volleyball club on weekends, and practices gentle morning yoga to keep her joints supple despite her occasional knee pain.
arts_persona: Minati Tandi delights in listening to classic bhajans, Urdu ghazals by Jagjit Singh, and Hindi TV serials starring Sakshi Tanwar, while she also crafts embroidered tablecloths inspired by traditional motifs, reflecting her love for both melody and handiwork.
travel_persona: Minati Tandi prefers short family trips by train, having visited Puri’s Jagannath Temple and the sun‑kissed beaches of Konark, she dreams of a modest pilgrimage to Varanasi during the winter, but her tight budget and household duties keep most journeys local and well‑planned.
culinary_persona: Minati Tandi’s palate celebrates a fusion of Odia and Mughlai flavors, often preparing dalma with a hint of saffron biryani spice, serving pakhala with mustard seeds and paneer cubes, and she especially enjoys dishes featuring coconut, cardamom, and ghee.
persona: Minati Tandi is a methodical, community‑oriented 49‑year‑old who balances an unyielding love for traditional cooking with relentless worry over every household detail.
cultural_background: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old Hindu woman living in the urban town of Khordha in Odisha. Though Khordha is predominantly Odia-speaking, her family has retained Urdu as its mother tongue, a legacy of migration from North-India generations ago. She grew up immersed in a blend of Odia cultural customs - such as Raja Parba, Durga Puja and the sweet delicacy rasgulla - and the linguistic flavour of Urdu poetry and household rhythms. This hybrid cultural setting shapes her daily life, from the way she prepares food that fuses Odia and Mughlai flavours to her participation in local temple festivals while still cherishing Urdu folk songs at home.
linguistic_background: Minati's first language is Urdu; she speaks it fluently and uses it at home for everyday conversation, storytelling, and listening to Urdu poetry on the radio. She also understands and converses in Hindi, which she picked up through schoolmates and community interactions, though her formal education was limited, leaving her illiterate. Consequently, she cannot read or write Urdu, Hindi, or Odia scripts, relying instead on oral communication and assistance from family members for written tasks such as bills or prescriptions.
skills_and_expertise: Minati’s expertise lies in comprehensive household management. She skillfully plans meals, often blending Odia and Mughlai cuisine, and ensures the family’s nutritional needs are met. Her budgeting abilities help the household navigate expenses within a modest income. She organizes family events and religious celebrations, coordinating decorations, food preparation, and guest arrangements. Minati also possesses practical first‑aid knowledge, basic sewing and embroidery skills, and the ability to mobilize neighbors for community initiatives, reflecting her organized and reliable nature.
skills_and_expertise_list: [ 'Household management', 'Cooking Odia and Mughlai dishes', 'Budgeting household expenses', 'Organizing family events and religious celebrations', 'Basic first aid', 'Sewing and embroidery', 'Community coordination and mobilization' ]
hobbies_and_interests: Minati enjoys cooking traditional recipes, especially experimenting with Odia and Mughlai flavors. She has a small garden where she grows herbs and seasonal vegetables. She loves attending community gatherings and temple festivals, listening to bhajans and Urdu poetry on the radio. Sewing, embroidery, and creating simple handicrafts keep her hands busy. In leisure, she watches popular Hindi TV serials and occasionally participates in local dance events during festivals.
hobbies_and_interests_list: [ 'Cooking traditional recipes', 'Gardening herbs and seasonal vegetables', 'Attending community gatherings and temple festivals', 'Listening to bhajans and Urdu poetry', 'Sewing and embroidery', 'Watching Hindi TV serials', 'Participating in local dance events during festivals' ]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Minati aspires to become literate by enrolling in adult education classes, which would enable her to manage household paperwork independently and perhaps start a small home‑based catering service specializing in her signature Odia‑Mughlai fusion dishes. She also wishes to support her children's further education, mentor younger women in her neighbourhood through a self‑help group, and continue to be a respected elder who preserves and passes on cultural traditions.
persona_clinical_summary: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old female currently married, retired homemaker with an illiterate education level living in an urban zone of Odisha. Her first language is Urdu, and she is a community-oriented individual who manages household responsibilities. She is a methodical person who expresses concern over family details and enjoys listening to Urdu poetry and bhajans.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Urdu (code=ur, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Odisha, district=Khordha.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Urdu.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 49
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Odisha
- district: Khordha
- first_language: Urdu
- clinical_persona_summary: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old female currently married, retired homemaker with an illiterate education level living in an urban zone of Odisha. Her first language is Urdu, and she is a community-oriented individual who manages household responsibilities. She is a methodical person who expresses concern over family details and enjoys listening to Urdu poetry and bhajans.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
امیجنگ رپورٹ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] [[AGE|49]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | رپورٹ کردہ ڈاکٹر [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
نتائج: سینے کا سادہ ریڈیوگراف [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] پر کیا گیا جو [[DISTRICT|Khordha]] ضلع کی 49 سالہ خاتون ہیں۔ معائنہ [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]] میں 15 اگست 2024 کو کیا گیا۔ یہ مطالعہ ڈاکٹر ایس. کے. مشرا کے حکم پر کیا گیا تھا۔ پھیپھڑوں کے حصے صاف ہیں اور کوئی شدید انفلٹریٹس، کنسولیڈیشن، یا پلورل ایفیوژن موجود نہیں ہے۔ کارڈیک سلیوٹ نارمل حدود کے اندر ہے۔ ہڈیوں والا سینہ (بونی تھوریکس) برقرار ہے اور کوئی شدید فریکچر نہیں ہے۔ کوئی شدید ہڈیوں کی غیر معمولی حالت نہیں پائی گئی۔ کوسٹو فرینک اینگلز تیز اور صاف ہیں۔ ٹریکیا درمیان میں ہے۔ نرم بافتیں (سافٹ ٹشوز) غیر معمولی نہیں ہیں۔ تاثر: سینے کے ریڈیوگراف پر کوئی شدید کارڈیو پلمونری غیر معمولی حالت نہیں پائی گئی۔ رپورٹ مریض کے لیے تیار کی گئی ہے جو [[DISTRICT|Khordha]] ضلع، اوڈیشہ میں مقیم ہے۔ مریض کا [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] اس ملاقات سے منسلک ہے۔ فالو اپ کے لیے مریض سے [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437012345]] پر رابطہ کیا جا سکتا ہے۔ رپورٹ [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-OD-001]] کے تحت فائل کی گئی ہے۔
generated_text_en:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] [[AGE|49]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
Findings: Plain radiograph of the chest was performed on [[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] who is a 49-year-old female from [[DISTRICT|Khordha]] district. The examination was conducted at [[HOSPITAL_NAME|SCB Medical College Hospital]] on 15 August 2024. The study was ordered by Dr. S. K. Mishra. The lung fields are clear without any acute infiltrates, consolidation, or pleural effusion. The cardiac silhouette is within normal limits. The bony thorax is intact without any acute fractures. No acute osseous abnormalities are identified. The costophrenic angles are sharp and clear. The trachea is midline. The soft tissues are unremarkable. Impression: No acute cardiopulmonary abnormality identified on the chest radiograph. The report was generated for the patient residing in [[DISTRICT|Khordha]] district, Odisha. The patient's [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]] is linked to this encounter. The patient can be reached at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9437012345]] for follow-up. The report is filed under [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-OD-001]].
judge_reasoning: Urdu-script prose matches expected language; all TYPEs allowed; female age 49 fits chest X-ray; Latin IDs in tags permitted; no mismatches or violations.

professional_persona: Minati Tandi is a seasoned household manager and aspiring home‑catering entrepreneur who meticulously plans meals, budgets the family expenses, and coordinates community celebrations, all while her innate need for structure sometimes leads her to over‑prepare and stress over every detail.
linguistic_persona: Minati Tandi speaks fluent Urdu at home, comfortably converses in Hindi, and, being illiterate, relies on family members to handle any reading or writing in Odia, Urdu or Hindi, which she navigates with a sharp memory and a habit of double‑checking spoken instructions.
sports_persona: Minati Tandi enjoys watching cricket, especially rooting for the Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, joins the local women’s volleyball club on weekends, and practices gentle morning yoga to keep her joints supple despite her occasional knee pain.
arts_persona: Minati Tandi delights in listening to classic bhajans, Urdu ghazals by Jagjit Singh, and Hindi TV serials starring Sakshi Tanwar, while she also crafts embroidered tablecloths inspired by traditional motifs, reflecting her love for both melody and handiwork.
travel_persona: Minati Tandi prefers short family trips by train, having visited Puri’s Jagannath Temple and the sun‑kissed beaches of Konark, she dreams of a modest pilgrimage to Varanasi during the winter, but her tight budget and household duties keep most journeys local and well‑planned.
culinary_persona: Minati Tandi’s palate celebrates a fusion of Odia and Mughlai flavors, often preparing dalma with a hint of saffron biryani spice, serving pakhala with mustard seeds and paneer cubes, and she especially enjoys dishes featuring coconut, cardamom, and ghee.
persona: Minati Tandi is a methodical, community‑oriented 49‑year‑old who balances an unyielding love for traditional cooking with relentless worry over every household detail.
cultural_background: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old Hindu woman living in the urban town of Khordha in Odisha. Though Khordha is predominantly Odia-speaking, her family has retained Urdu as its mother tongue, a legacy of migration from North-India generations ago. She grew up immersed in a blend of Odia cultural customs - such as Raja Parba, Durga Puja and the sweet delicacy rasgulla - and the linguistic flavour of Urdu poetry and household rhythms. This hybrid cultural setting shapes her daily life, from the way she prepares food that fuses Odia and Mughlai flavours to her participation in local temple festivals while still cherishing Urdu folk songs at home.
linguistic_background: Minati's first language is Urdu; she speaks it fluently and uses it at home for everyday conversation, storytelling, and listening to Urdu poetry on the radio. She also understands and converses in Hindi, which she picked up through schoolmates and community interactions, though her formal education was limited, leaving her illiterate. Consequently, she cannot read or write Urdu, Hindi, or Odia scripts, relying instead on oral communication and assistance from family members for written tasks such as bills or prescriptions.
skills_and_expertise: Minati’s expertise lies in comprehensive household management. She skillfully plans meals, often blending Odia and Mughlai cuisine, and ensures the family’s nutritional needs are met. Her budgeting abilities help the household navigate expenses within a modest income. She organizes family events and religious celebrations, coordinating decorations, food preparation, and guest arrangements. Minati also possesses practical first‑aid knowledge, basic sewing and embroidery skills, and the ability to mobilize neighbors for community initiatives, reflecting her organized and reliable nature.
skills_and_expertise_list: [ 'Household management', 'Cooking Odia and Mughlai dishes', 'Budgeting household expenses', 'Organizing family events and religious celebrations', 'Basic first aid', 'Sewing and embroidery', 'Community coordination and mobilization' ]
hobbies_and_interests: Minati enjoys cooking traditional recipes, especially experimenting with Odia and Mughlai flavors. She has a small garden where she grows herbs and seasonal vegetables. She loves attending community gatherings and temple festivals, listening to bhajans and Urdu poetry on the radio. Sewing, embroidery, and creating simple handicrafts keep her hands busy. In leisure, she watches popular Hindi TV serials and occasionally participates in local dance events during festivals.
hobbies_and_interests_list: [ 'Cooking traditional recipes', 'Gardening herbs and seasonal vegetables', 'Attending community gatherings and temple festivals', 'Listening to bhajans and Urdu poetry', 'Sewing and embroidery', 'Watching Hindi TV serials', 'Participating in local dance events during festivals' ]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Minati aspires to become literate by enrolling in adult education classes, which would enable her to manage household paperwork independently and perhaps start a small home‑based catering service specializing in her signature Odia‑Mughlai fusion dishes. She also wishes to support her children's further education, mentor younger women in her neighbourhood through a self‑help group, and continue to be a respected elder who preserves and passes on cultural traditions.
persona_clinical_summary: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old female who is currently married and retired, residing in an urban area of Odisha. She is illiterate, speaks Urdu as her first language, and lives in Khordha district. Her lifestyle is characterized by a focus on household management and community involvement, with a traditional diet and limited travel.
system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ER Triage & Nursing Notes for clinical domain Maternal Health.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Urdu (code=ur, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Maternal Health — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Odisha, district=Khordha.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Urdu.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once in the arrival/RTA/ambulance line. Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 49
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Illiterate
- zone: Urban
- state: Odisha
- district: Khordha
- first_language: Urdu
- clinical_persona_summary: Minati Tandi is a 49-year-old female who is currently married and retired, residing in an urban area of Odisha. She is illiterate, speaks Urdu as her first language, and lives in Khordha district. Her lifestyle is characterized by a focus on household management and community involvement, with a traditional diet and limited travel.

Document format guide for ER Triage & Nursing Notes (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|...]]
Arrived by ambulance / RTA vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|PB10AB1234]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|...]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Vitals / acuity: <short notes>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
ای آر ٹریاج — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Khordha District Hospital]] مقابلہ [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] [[AGE|49]] [[GENDER|Female]] وارڈ [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] بستر [[BED_NUMBER|07]]
ایمبولینس گاڑی [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|OD10C1234567]] کے ذریعے پہنچے (بالکل ایک بار)۔
رشتہ دار [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sanjay Tandi]] فون [[PHONE_NUMBER|9438901234]] ڈاکٹر [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
حیاتی علامات / شدت: مریضہ ہوش میں، ہوشیار، ہلکی تکلیف میں ہے۔ بی پی 140/90 mmHg، ایچ آر 110/منٹ، آر آر 22/منٹ، کمرہ کی ہوا پر SpO2 97%، درجہ حرارت 37.8°C۔ پیٹ کے نچلے حصے میں درد اور اندام نہانی سے خون بہنے کی شکایت۔

نرسنگ نوٹس:
مریضہ منتی تندی، 49 سالہ خاتون، ایمبولینس کے ذریعے 6 گھنٹے سے پیٹ کے نچلے حصے میں درد اور اندام نہانی سے خون بہنے کی بنیادی شکایت کے ساتھ پیش ہوئیں۔ درد کو مستقل، مدھم اور پیٹ کے اوپری حصے (suprapubic region) میں مرکوز بتایا گیا ہے، جس کی شدت 6/10 ہے۔ خون بہنا چمکدار سرخ، معتدل مقدار میں ہے، اور کسی لوتھڑے کا ذکر نہیں کیا گیا۔ مریضہ چکر آنے اور کمزوری محسوس کرنے کی اطلاع دیتی ہیں۔

معائنے پر، پیٹ نرم ہے لیکن نچلے حصوں میں چھونے پر درد محسوس ہوتا ہے۔ رحم کا فنڈس (uterine fundus) ہلکا سا دردناک ہے۔ پیریٹونائٹس (peritonitis) کی کوئی علامت نہیں ہے۔ اسپیکولم معائنے سے ایک پھیلا ہوا سروائیکل اوس (dilated cervical os) ظاہر ہوتا ہے جس سے فعال خون بہہ رہا ہے۔

مریضہ این پی او (NPO) ہے۔ دو بڑے بور آئی وی کینولا (IV cannulas) لگائے گئے ہیں۔ 500 ملی لیٹر نارمل سیلائن بولس شروع کیا گیا۔ سی بی سی، کوایگولیشن پروفائل، اور بلڈ گروپ کے لیے خون کے نمونے بھیجے گئے۔ 2 یونٹ پیکڈ ریڈ بلڈ سیلز کے لیے ٹائپ اینڈ کراس میچ۔ مسلسل فیٹل مانیٹرنگ شروع کر دی گئی ہے؛ فیٹل ہارٹ ریٹ 140/منٹ، ری ایکٹو ہے۔

ڈاکٹر ڈاکٹر ایس۔ کے۔ مشرا کو مطلع کر دیا گیا ہے اور وہ قبل از زچگی خون بہنے (antepartum hemorrhage) کی ممکنہ وجہ کے لیے تشخیص کر رہے ہیں۔ مریضہ کو مزید تحقیقات اور انتظام کے لیے تیار کیا جا رہا ہے۔ رشتہ دار سنجے تندی کو مریضہ کی حالت کے بارے میں آگاہ کر دیا گیا ہے۔
generated_text_en:
ER triage — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Khordha District Hospital]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-2024-0815-001]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Minati Tandi]] [[AGE|49]] [[GENDER|Female]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|ER]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|07]]
Arrived by ambulance vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|OD10C1234567]] (exactly once).
Relative [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sanjay Tandi]] Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|9438901234]] Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. K. Mishra]]
Vitals / acuity: Patient conscious, alert, in mild distress. BP 140/90 mmHg, HR 110/min, RR 22/min, SpO2 97% on room air, Temp 37.8°C. Complaint of lower abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding.

Nursing Notes:
Patient Minati Tandi, a 49-year-old female, presented via ambulance with chief complaint of lower abdominal pain and vaginal bleeding for the past 6 hours. The pain is described as constant, dull, and localized to the suprapubic region, rated 6/10 in severity. The bleeding is bright red, moderate in amount, with no clots noted. Patient reports feeling dizzy and weak.

On examination, the abdomen is soft but tender to palpation in the lower quadrants. The uterine fundus is mildly tender. No signs of peritonitis. Speculum examination reveals a dilated cervical os with active bleeding.

Patient is NPO. Two large-bore IV cannulas established. 500ml of Normal Saline bolus initiated. Blood samples sent for CBC, coagulation profile, and blood group. Type and crossmatch for 2 units of packed red blood cells. Continuous fetal monitoring initiated; fetal heart rate 140/min, reactive.

Doctor Dr. S. K. Mishra has been informed and is evaluating for possible cause of antepartum hemorrhage. Patient is being prepared for further investigations and management. Relative Sanjay Tandi has been updated on the patient's condition.
judge_reasoning: Urdu-script prose matches expected language; all TYPEs allowed; Latin tags and medical terms permitted; age 49 fits reproductive-age maternal definition; no length, shift, or plausibility issues.
